questions.statement:
<p>In the sentence, identify the segment which contains the grammatical error.</p>

<p>India export 12.01 million tonnes of rice and four million tonnes of sugar in the past five years.</p>

Identify that part of the given sentence which is not acceptable in standard written English :<br />
The new policy will effect all employees starting next month.
questions.explanation:
The correct answer is '2' i.e. effect .

Key Points

  • The given sentence contains an incorrect usage of the word "effect."
  • "Effect" is a noun that means "a result or outcome." However, the sentence requires the verb "affect," which means "to influence or make an impact."
  • The correct word in this context should be "affect."

Correct sentence: The new policy will affect all employees starting next month.

Additional Information

  • Usage of "Affect" and "Effect":
    • Affect (verb): To influence or have an impact on something. Example: The weather affects my mood.
    • Effect (noun): A result or outcome. Example: The new law had a significant effect on the economy.
  • Always double-check the context of the sentence to determine whether a verb (affect) or a noun (effect) is needed.
